title: ONGC Physics — Full-length Mock 06
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Stokes' theorem relates a surface integral of the curl of a vector field to a line integral around the boundary. Which identity is correct?
questions.solution: Stokes': ∮F·dr = ∬(∇×F)·dA. Option B is the divergence theorem.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Green's theorem in the plane relates a line integral around a closed curve C to a double integral over the enclosed region D. For F = (P, Q):
questions.solution: Green's: ∮(Pdx+Qdy) = ∬(∂Q/∂x−∂P/∂y)dA. The 2D special case of Stokes' theorem.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The divergence theorem (Gauss's theorem) converts a surface integral into a volume integral. Which statement is correct?
options: ∬_S F·dA = ∭_V (∇·F) dV — requires S to be open
∬_S F·dA = ∭_V (∇·F) dV — S must be closed, bounding a simply connected volume V
∬_S F·dA = ∭_V (∇²F) dV for any vector field
∬_S F·dA = ∭_V (∇×F) dV for any vector field
questions.solution: Requires S closed, bounding simply connected V, with F continuously differentiable.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3×3 matrix A has eigenvalues 1, 2, and 3. The eigenvalues of A² are:
questions.solution: If Av = λv then A²v = λ²v. Squares of 1, 2, 3 are 1, 4, 9.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Euler angles (φ, θ, ψ) describe rigid body orientation. The angle θ represents:
options: The angle between the space-fixed Z-axis and the body-fixed z-axis (nutation)
The precession angle about the space-fixed Z-axis
questions.solution: θ is nutation: angle between space Z and body z. φ is precession, ψ is spin.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A torque-free symmetric top (I₁ = I₂ ≠ I₃) precesses about its angular momentum. The half-angle α of the precession cone satisfies:
questions.solution: cos α = I₃ω₃/L where ω₃ is spin along symmetry axis, L is conserved angular momentum.

figure.caption: Angular velocity precesses around fixed angular momentum

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Two identical masses m connected by three springs: wall—k—m—k—m—k—wall. The angular frequency of the antisymmetric mode is:
questions.solution: Antisymmetric mode: middle spring maximally compressed. ω² = 3k/m.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Hubble's law v = H₀d describes cosmic expansion. The Hubble time 1/H₀ represents:
options: The age of the universe in all cosmological models
A characteristic expansion time; approximately the age for linear expansion
The time for light to cross the observable universe
questions.solution: t_H = 1/H₀ ≈ 14.4 Gyr. For linear expansion, age = 1/H₀.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A rectangular cavity a × b × d has TE₁₀₁ resonant frequency. For a = 3 cm, d = 4 cm, the frequency is closest to:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: When unpolarized light hits a glass surface (n = 1.5) at the Brewster angle, the reflected light is:
options: Completely polarized perpendicular to the plane of incidence (s-polarized)
Completely polarized in the plane of incidence (p-polarized)
questions.solution: At Brewster's angle, p-component has zero reflection. Only s-polarized light is reflected.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Faraday rotation in a magnetized plasma rotates the polarization plane. The rotation angle is proportional to:
questions.solution: θ_F ∝ n_e·B·L/ω². Depends on electron density, B along propagation, and path length.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Cherenkov radiation is emitted when a charged particle exceeds c/n in a medium. The emission angle θ satisfies:
questions.solution: cos θ = c/(nv) = 1/(nβ). Threshold: nβ > 1.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For a particle in an infinite potential well of width L, the energy of the n = 4 state is how many times the ground state energy?
questions.solution: Eₙ ∝ n². E₄/E₁ = 16.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The degeneracy of the first excited state (N = 1) of a 3D isotropic harmonic oscillator is:
questions.solution: Three configurations: (1,0,0), (0,1,0), (0,0,1). Degeneracy = 3.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Spin-orbit coupling in hydrogen splits the 2p state (l = 1, s = 1/2). The two possible values of j are:
questions.solution: j ranges |l−s| to l+s = 1/2, 3/2.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For the Bell state |Φ⁺⟩ = (|00⟩+|11⟩)/√2, the maximum CHSH parameter S is:
questions.solution: Local hidden variables: |S| ≤ 2. QM: S = 2√2 ≈ 2.83.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Van der Waals equation (P + a/V²)(V − b) = RT. Constants a and b represent:
options: a: molecular volume, b: intermolecular attraction
a: intermolecular attraction, b: excluded volume
a: temperature correction, b: pressure correction
a: kinetic energy, b: potential energy
questions.solution: 'a' corrects for attractive forces. 'b' corrects for finite molecular volume.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Debye model predicts that at very low temperatures (T ≪ Θ_D), the lattice heat capacity is proportional to:
questions.solution: Debye T³ law: C_V ∝ T³ at low T. From g(ω) ∝ ω² density of states.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For a 3D ideal Bose gas, the BEC critical temperature T_c depends on density n as:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Onsager reciprocal relations state that the transport coefficient matrix satisfies:
questions.solution: L_ij = L_ji follows from microscopic reversibility.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Lamb shift lifts the 2S₁/₂–2P₁/₂ degeneracy. It is primarily caused by:
options: QED radiative corrections (vacuum polarization + electron self-energy)
questions.solution: QED effects: vacuum polarization and self-energy shift 2S₁/₂ upward.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Atomic & Molecular Physics · Anomalous Zeeman Effect
questions.stem: For a ²D₃/₂ term (L=2, S=1/2, J=3/2), the Landé g-factor is:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In rotational Raman spectroscopy of a linear molecule, the selection rule for J is:
questions.solution: Rotational Raman: ΔJ = ±2. Arises from rank-2 polarizability tensor.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Atomic & Molecular Physics · Laser Rate Equations
questions.stem: In a four-level laser, population inversion requires:
options: N₃ > N₂ (upper laser level more populated than lower)
questions.solution: N₃ > N₂. Rapid decay |2⟩→|1⟩ keeps N₂ low.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For a 2D square lattice (lattice constant a), the reciprocal lattice vectors are:
questions.solution: From aᵢ·bⱼ = 2πδᵢⱼ: b₁=(2π/a)x̂, b₂=(2π/a)ŷ.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Bloch's theorem ψ_k(r) = e^(ik·r) u_k(r) implies that in a periodic potential:
options: Electrons are localized at atomic sites
Energy eigenvalues form continuous bands indexed by crystal momentum k
questions.solution: Bloch's theorem gives energy bands E_n(k) separated by gaps — foundation of band theory.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: At T = 0, the Fermi energy of a free electron gas (density n) is:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: BCS theory predicts the superconducting gap ratio at T = 0:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Magnetic domains form in ferromagnetic materials primarily to minimize:
questions.solution: Domains minimize magnetostatic energy from the external field of a uniformly magnetized sample.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: For an n-channel enhancement MOSFET, the saturation region occurs when:
options: V_GS > V_th and V_DS < V_GS − V_th
V_GS > V_th and V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th
questions.solution: Saturation: V_GS > V_th AND V_DS ≥ V_GS − V_th (channel pinched off at drain).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A CMOS inverter has PMOS (top) and NMOS (bottom). When input is HIGH (V_DD):
questions.solution: NMOS V_GS = V_DD > V_th (conducts). PMOS V_SG = 0 (off). Output pulled LOW.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In an astable 555 timer, the oscillation frequency depends on:
questions.solution: f = 1.44/((R₁+2R₂)C). Capacitor charges through R₁+R₂, discharges through R₂.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A parallel-in serial-out (PISO) shift register converts:
questions.solution: PISO loads data in parallel and shifts out serially.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A successive approximation (SAR) ADC converts an analog input by:
options: Binary search: testing bits from MSB to LSB
questions.solution: Binary search: tests each bit MSB→LSB. n bits require n comparisons.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Nuclear & Particle Physics · Nuclear Magnetic Moments
questions.stem: The deuteron magnetic moment is 0.857 μ_N. For a pure S-state proton-neutron pair it would be:
options: 0.880 μ_N (sum of p and n moments)
questions.solution: Pure S-state: μ_d = μ_p + μ_n = 2.793 + (−1.913) = 0.880 μ_N. Observed difference indicates D-state admixture.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Nuclear & Particle Physics · Beta Decay V-A Theory
questions.stem: The V−A theory of weak interactions means the W boson couples to:
questions.solution: V−A: W couples to left-handed particles and right-handed antiparticles. Maximal parity violation.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Q-value of a nuclear reaction is defined as:
questions.solution: Q = KE(products) − KE(reactants) = [M(reactants)−M(products)]c².

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The threshold photon energy for pair production (γ → e⁺e⁻) near a nucleus is:
questions.solution: E_min = 2m_e c² = 1.022 MeV.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: How many quark flavors exist in the Standard Model?
questions.solution: Six: (up,down), (charm,strange), (top,bottom). Top quark ~173 GeV/c².

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Wu experiment (1957) demonstrated parity violation by observing:
options: Asymmetric β-electron emission from polarized ⁶⁰Co nuclei
questions.solution: β-electrons from polarized ⁶⁰Co emitted opposite to nuclear spin.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A:B=2:3, B:C=4:5. Then A:B:C =

figure.caption: Bar representation of ratio 3:2
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Ratio Visualization</text><rect x="60" y="65" width="120" height="40" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><rect x="60" y="120" width="80" height="40" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#ef4444" rx="4"/><text x="120" y="90" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">3 parts</text><text x="100" y="145" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">2 parts</text><text x="300" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">A : B = 3 : 2</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A completes in 12 days, B in 18 days. Together:

figure.caption: Work rates: combined effort reduces time
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Work Rate Diagram</text><rect x="80" y="55" width="160" height="30" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><rect x="80" y="100" width="120" height="30" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#22c55e" rx="4"/><rect x="80" y="145" width="200" height="30" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="4"/><text x="160" y="75" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">A: 1/6 per day</text><text x="140" y="120" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">B: 1/8 per day</text><text x="180" y="165" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">Combined: 1/6+1/8 = 7/24</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Average of 5 consecutive odd numbers is 27. Largest:
questions.solution: Numbers: 23,25,27,29,31. Largest = 31.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two dice thrown. P(sum=7):
questions.solution: 6 favorable out of 36. P=1/6.

figure.caption: Venn diagram for probability union
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Probability Venn</text><circle cx="170" cy="110" r="55" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="300" cy="110" r="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/><text x="145" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">P(A)</text><text x="325" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" font-weight="bold">P(B)</text><text x="235" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">A∩B</text><text x="240" y="185" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">P(A∪B)=P(A)+P(B)−P(A∩B)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If x+1/x=5, then x²+1/x² =

figure.caption: Common identities
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Algebraic Identities</text><text x="240" y="65" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">(a+b)² = a²+2ab+b²</text><text x="240" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">(a−b)² = a²−2ab+b²</text><text x="240" y="145" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="13" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">a²−b² = (a+b)(a−b)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: APPLE reversed is ELPPA. ORANGE reversed is:
questions.solution: Pattern: reverse the word. ORANGE → EGNARO.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A says: 'I am the only son of your mother's father.' A is B's:
questions.solution: B's maternal grandfather's only son = B's mother's brother = maternal uncle.

figure.caption: Family tree for blood relation problems
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Family Tree</text><circle cx="160" cy="50" r="16" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="320" cy="50" r="16" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="176" y1="50" x2="304" y2="50" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="240" y1="50" x2="240" y2="85" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="160" cy="120" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="240" cy="120" r="14" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="320" cy="120" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="160" y2="106"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="240" y2="106"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="320" y2="106"/><text x="160" y="54" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">M</text><text x="320" y="54" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">F</text><text x="160" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">S</text><text x="240" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">D</text><text x="320" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">S</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: All dogs are animals. Some animals are cats. Does 'Some dogs are cats' follow?
questions.solution: The 'some animals' that are cats need not include any dogs.

figure.caption: Venn diagram for syllogism
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Syllogism Venn</text><circle cx="180" cy="110" r="50" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="300" cy="110" r="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/><text x="165" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6">All A</text><text x="315" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e">Some B</text><text x="240" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Overlap?</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A,B,C,D,E in a row north. C right of A. B at one end. E left of D. D not next to B. Left end:
questions.solution: Arrangement: E D A C B. Left end = E.

figure.caption: Logic grid for constraint puzzles

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: P,Q,R,S at square table. P left of Q. R opposite P. Right of R:
questions.solution: Clockwise: Q,P,S,R. S is right of R.

questions.stem: Riya: 10 km N, right 5 km, right 10 km. Distance from start:
questions.solution: N10, E5, S10. Net = 5 km East.

questions.stem: 120m + 180m trains, 54 + 72 km/h opposite. Cross in:

figure.caption: Train and platform crossing
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Train Crossing Platform</text><rect x="40" y="80" width="180" height="30" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><line x1="40" y1="125" x2="440" y2="125" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="250" y="80" width="180" height="30" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="4"/><text x="130" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Train (L)</text><text x="340" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Platform (P)</text><text x="240" y="170" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle">Time = (L+P)/speed</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If DELHI is coded as CCIDD (each letter shifts back by its position), MUMBAI is:
questions.solution: D→C(−1), E→C(−2), L→I(−3), H→D(−4), I→D(−5). Applying same pattern to MUMBAI.

figure.caption: Letter-to-letter coding pattern
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Letter Code</text><text x="160" y="70" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">A  B  C  D  E  F  G</text><text x="160" y="90" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#f59e0b">↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓</text><text x="160" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#ef4444" font-weight="bold">Z  Y  X  W  V  U  T</text><text x="240" y="160" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">Reverse cipher: A→Z, B→Y, ...</text></svg>

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Nodal agency for petroleum and natural gas in India:
options: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
questions.solution: MoPNG is responsible for oil and gas policy.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC was founded in:
questions.solution: ONGC established August 14, 1956.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Bombay High offshore oil field is in which basin:
questions.solution: Mumbai High, ~160 km west of Mumbai.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Indian satellite series for communication:
questions.solution: Both INSAT and GSAT for communication/broadcasting.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Paris Agreement aims to limit warming to:
questions.solution: Well below 2°C, efforts toward 1.5°C.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC headquarters:
questions.solution: Headquartered in Dehradun, Uttarakhand.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's Strategic Petroleum Reserves managed by:
questions.solution: ISPRL manages reserves at Visakhapatnam, Mangalore, Padur.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first nuclear power station:
questions.solution: Tarapur (TAPS), Maharashtra, 1969.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's largest oil-producing state:
questions.solution: Rajasthan since 2009 (Barmer fields).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Richter scale measures:
questions.solution: Earthquake magnitude from seismic waves.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Most similar to 'ABUNDANT':
questions.solution: Abundant = existing in large quantities; plentiful.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Most opposite to 'BENIGN':
questions.solution: Benign = gentle. Malignant = harmful/cancerous.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Grammatically correct sentence:
options: Neither the teacher nor the students was present.
Each of the boys have completed their assignment.
The committee has decided to approve the proposal.
questions.solution: 'The committee has decided' — collective noun, singular verb.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: 'To burn the midnight oil' means:
questions.solution: Work or study late into the night.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: A person who loves books is a:
questions.solution: Bibliophile = book lover.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The meeting is _____ Monday.'
questions.solution: Use 'on' with days of the week.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Read: 'The monsoon is driven by differential heating of land and sea.' What drives it?
options: Differential heating of land and sea
questions.solution: The passage states differential heating of land and sea.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: 'By the time we arrived, the train _____.'
questions.solution: Past perfect for action completed before another past action.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Most similar to 'ELOQUENT':
questions.solution: Eloquent = fluent, persuasive, expressive.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: She has been working here _____ 2019.
questions.solution: 'Since' with a specific point in time.
